sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 2-3 week history of right shoulder pain, which worsened after working on various projects in his backyard, including lifting heavy objects.
He describes the pain as sore and tender on the outside of the shoulder, particularly when raising his arm.
He has had some previous issues with this shoulder from playing baseball as a child, but this is a new and persistent problem.
The pain is exacerbated by movement and improved with rest and Advil.
He also experiences some pain at night, likely due to sleeping on the affected side.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing baseball, which resulted in previous shoulder pain.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness in his arm or hands.
Current medications: I prescribed Tylenol for pain management.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 116/84, heart rate 62, respirations 16, temperature 97.8, oxygen saturation 99%.
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion in the right shoulder, with tenderness at the greater tuberosity of the humerus.
There is no tenderness at the sternoclavicular or acromioclavicular joints.
The patient has a good hand grip, and on neurovascular exam of the right arm, capillary refill is less than 3 seconds and sensation to touch is intact.
Test Results: The patient's shoulder X-ray shows no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy, which is an injury to the tendons of the muscles that help make the shoulder muscles.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; active; confirmed.
Plan:
I referred the patient to physical therapy for approximately 6-8 weeks to address the rotator cuff tendinopathy.
I also ordered an MRI to further evaluate the condition.
During this time, I recommend the patient continue taking Tylenol for pain management.
If symptoms do not improve, we may consider a steroid injection for further relief.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after physical therapy to assess progress and adjust the treatment plan as needed.
